edge_hypothesis: Bollinger Band breakouts preceded by bandwidth compression (squeeze) are higher quality than random BB breaks. When BB width contracts below its 20-bar average, the subsequent expansion produces directional moves with momentum. Volume confirmation (>1.2x 20-bar avg) validates the breakout is institutional, not noise. This is a volatility-cycle strategy that exploits the compression-expansion pattern rather than pure momentum.
regime_assumption: TRANSITIONAL (ranging→trending). Best during regime transitions when volatility expands after compression. ADX rising from <20 to >20 is the ideal entry window. Invalidate if breakout trades show no better PF than random BB touches.
invalidation_conditions: Squeeze-filtered entries show no improvement over unfiltered BB breakout

variants.name: bb_squeeze_vol_confirm
variants.description: Bollinger breakout only after bandwidth squeeze with strong volume confirmation (>1.5x avg). Tests whether compression+volume produces high-quality breakout entries.
entry_long: Close breaks above BBU_20_2.0 (upper Bollinger Band).
entry_short: Close breaks below BBL_20_2.0 (lower Bollinger Band).
filters: Backtester executable indicators: EMA, SMA, RSI, ATR, MACD, Bollinger Bands, Stochastic, ADX, CCI, Williams %R, OBV, VWAP, Ichimoku, Supertrend, Donchian Channels.
Regime gate: Volatility expansion after squeeze (BB bandwidth compression)
exit_rules: Stop loss at 2.5x ATR below entry (wide stop for breakout volatility).
Take profit at 4.0x ATR above entry.
Exit if price re-enters BB middle band (BBM_20) — breakout failed.
Exit on max 25 bars in trade.
Hypothesis failure mode guard: False breakout reversal within 3 bars
Hypothesis failure mode guard: Volume spike without follow-through
risk_rules: Wide stop (2.5x ATR) accommodates breakout volatility expansion.

components.notes: Volume ratio as primary confirmation (RSI as template framework role)
ATR_14 for stop/TP sizing and volatility reference

variants.name: bb_adx_expansion
variants.description: Bollinger breakout gated by ADX rising above 20 (regime transition from ranging to trending). Tests whether ADX expansion timing improves breakout quality.
entry_long: ADX_14 > 20 AND ADX_14 rising (current > previous bar).
entry_short: ADX_14 > 20 AND ADX_14 rising (current > previous bar).
filters: Backtester executable indicators: EMA, SMA, RSI, ATR, MACD, Bollinger Bands, Stochastic, ADX, CCI, Williams %R, OBV, VWAP, Ichimoku, Supertrend, Donchian Channels.
exit_rules: Stop loss at 2.0x ATR below entry.
Take profit at 3.5x ATR above entry.
Exit if ADX drops below 18 (trend losing strength).
Exit on max 20 bars in trade.
Hypothesis failure mode guard: ADX rises briefly then collapses
Hypothesis failure mode guard: Breakout trapped in wider consolidation
risk_rules: ADX-based exit provides dynamic risk management.

components.notes: ADX rising as regime transition confirmation

variants.name: bb_squeeze_1h_frequent
variants.description: Higher-frequency 1h BB breakout with lighter volume gate. Tests whether more frequent squeeze-breakout cycles on 1h accumulate edge through trade count.
filters: Backtester executable indicators: EMA, SMA, RSI, ATR, MACD, Bollinger Bands, Stochastic, ADX, CCI, Williams %R, OBV, VWAP, Ichimoku, Supertrend, Donchian Channels.
Regime gate: Volume above average (basic participation filter)
exit_rules: Stop loss at 2.0x ATR below entry.
Take profit at 3.0x ATR above entry (R:R 1:1.5).
Exit if price returns inside Bollinger Bands for 2 consecutive bars.
Exit on max 15 bars in trade.
Hypothesis failure mode guard: 1h noise producing many false breakouts
Hypothesis failure mode guard: Fee drag on high trade frequency
risk_rules: Reduced risk for higher frequency timeframe.

components.notes: BBU/BBL_20_2.0 breakout on 1h for higher frequency signals
